name: Twice Cooked Pork and Spicy Vegetables
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Pork butt, in one piece
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover the pork butt with water in a saucepan and bring to a boil, then simmer for 30 minutes. Add more hot water if the level drops below the pork.
2. Cool the pork in its cooking water. Wash mushrooms and soak them in warm water for 1 hour. Squeeze liquid from mushrooms and discard the stems, halving the mushrooms. Halve, seed, and core the bell pepper; cut into pieces about 1 inch by 1½ inches. Slice the bamboo to match the bell pepper size. Peel and slice the carrot on a bias into ovals.
3. Remove the pork from its cooking water and parboil the carrots in hot water for 1 minute. Slice approximately two-thirds of the pork butt into rectangles the same size as the bell pepper pieces. Reserve the remaining one-third for another dish.
4. Strain the mushroom liquid and combine it with soy sauce, sugar, and salt to taste.
5. Drain the bean curd and rinse in cold water; slice the same size as the bell pepper.
6. Add oil to a very hot wok. When the oil begins to smoke, stir-fry the mushrooms, garlic, and ginger for 1 minute.
7. Add the bell pepper, bamboo shoots, carrots, and pork; stir-fry for 1 minute.
8. Add the bean curd and mushroom liquid; bring to a boil.
9. On medium heat, cover the wok and cook for 1-2 minutes until the bell pepper is bright green and crisp.
10. Push the ingredients to one side of the wok, restir the cornstarch paste, then dribble it into the liquid until thickened. Cook briefly while stirring.
11. Recombine with the remaining ingredients and serve.
